title: Leaked API Credentials
summary: An automated scanner flagged a live production API key committed to a public GitHub repo. A senior security lead has pulled you into a war room to drive containment.

persona.name: Priya Nair
persona.description: A calm but relentless security lead running containment. Expects you to think in terms of revoke, rotate, scope-of-access, and evidence. Loses patience with anyone who wants to 'investigate first' before revoking. Reassured by decisive containment and a clear blast-radius assessment.

stakes: The key has read/write access to the customer database. Every minute it stays live is a minute an attacker could be exfiltrating data — and a potential breach-disclosure obligation.
hidden_facts: The key was pushed 40 minutes ago and the repo is public.
Access logs show three calls from an unfamiliar IP in the last 10 minutes.
The key grants read/write to the primary customer database.
Rotation is one CLI command but requires also redeploying two dependent services.
objectives: Revoke the leaked credential immediately rather than investigate first
Assess the blast radius (what the key could access, signs of misuse)
Rotate the key and redeploy dependent services
Preserve evidence and flag potential breach-disclosure
opening_line: We have a live prod key sitting in a public repo right now. Walk me through exactly what you're doing, in order, starting now.
